evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Implement and maintain Data validation processes to improve pipeline accuracy
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (data validation, governance policy, accuracy) dominate with no explicit velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Design and implement scalable data models to support BI and analytics
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Retail sector with business-decision-facing analytics but no regulatory, financial reporting, or compliance language present.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Mentor the Junior Analytic Engineer and provide technical leadership for the sub-squad
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A named BI-focused sub-squad with a junior role to mentor and named tools in responsibilities indicate a mid-stage, growing data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Develop and optimize Data pipelines using dbt or Dataform, Apache Airflow, Apache Spark, BigQuery, and SQL-based transformations
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise tool stack (dbt/Dataform, Airflow, Spark, BigQuery, Great Expectations) gives a concrete picture of daily work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Collaborate with BI Developers to ensure dashboards are powered by performant datasets
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role is centred on serving BI developers and data engineers within the data function itself, the defining internal_data pattern.
evidence.autonomy_level: help provision, structure, and lead our BI-focused sub-squad
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Leading and mentoring a sub-squad is strategic, but much of the work is framed as collaborating with and supporting adjacent data engineers, giving mixed autonomy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool or AI infrastructure mention appears anywhere in the extracted JD text.
evidence.testing_framing: Implement and maintain Data validation processes to improve pipeline accuracy
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data validation and governance are named as owned responsibilities with explicit implementation verbs, not just tools in a requirements list.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Implement Data governance policy across various projects
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data validation and governance are a stated concern but framed as operational pipeline accuracy rather than compliance or regulatory risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design and implement scalable data models to support BI and analytics
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The JD mixes designing new scalable data models with optimizing and maintaining existing pipelines and infrastructure.
